purpose: Rebuild the documentation todo from executable source reality (CLI, scripts, task orchestration, tests) and resolve drift without blindly deleting docs. Every mismatch must explicitly decide whether docs change, code changes, or both.
goal: A maintainable, code-backed documentation baseline where command examples, architecture claims and fallback semantics are consistent, test-guarded, and explicitly lifecycle-labeled (active vs historical).

review_basis.review_goal: derive documentation tasks from code and test contracts, not from stale plans
notes: CLI entrypoint and dispatch reviewed: agent/cli/main.py, agent/cli/init_wizard.py, agent/cli/update.py, agent/cli/doctor.py, agent/cli_goals.py
Bootstrap path reviewed: scripts/install-ananta.sh, scripts/install-ananta.ps1, docs/setup/bootstrap-install.md, docs/setup/quickstart.md, docs/setup/ananta_init.md

Architecture/fallback claims reviewed against code and tests: docs/hub-fallback-and-reliability.md, architektur/README.md, agent/routes/tasks/autopilot_tick_engine.py, agent/services/task_execution_tracking_service.py, tests/test_tasks_autopilot.py
Track inventory drift observed: docs/status/active_and_completed_tracks.md references outdated todo mapping

code_observations: OpenAI-compatible init examples in bootstrap docs/scripts still use --base-url while init parser uses --endpoint-url.
User-facing output in agent/cli_goals.py still points to `python -m agent.cli_goals ...` although user docs position `ananta ...` as standard path.
README and docs contain mixed onboarding narratives (CLI-first no-Docker vs Docker-first quickstart) that need explicit path separation.
Fallback provenance vocabulary in docs (`delegated|hub_fallback`) differs from runtime/test values (`delegated_worker|hub_as_worker_fallback|fallback_blocked`).
Python minimum version guidance differs between docs (3.11+) and runtime checks/tooling (3.10+).
non_goals: Do not delete architecture/docs content without replacement pointers and lifecycle labels.
Do not rewrite product strategy docs that are not tied to executable behavior or operator workflows.
Do not break developer fallback entrypoints only to force wording consistency.
Do not introduce a second competing architecture model outside hub-owned orchestration.
success_criteria: All user-path command examples in README/setup/CLI docs are executable against current CLI contracts.
Every identified doc-code mismatch has an explicit decision: update_docs, update_code, or update_both with rationale.
Architecture and fallback docs use the same vocabulary as emitted runtime metadata, or compatibility aliases are documented and tested.
Historical docs are clearly labeled and linked to canonical replacements instead of silently drifting.
Documentation drift checks run in tests/release gate to prevent regression.

tasks.title: Executable command contract inventory add
acceptance_criteria: Inventory is generated from code-level parsers/dispatchers (`agent/cli/main.py`, `agent/cli/init_wizard.py`, `agent/cli/update.py`, `agent/cli/doctor.py`, `agent/cli_goals.py`).
Inventory distinguishes user command, compatibility command and developer-only fallback command groups.
Inventory includes key option contracts (e.g., endpoint-url vs base-url) and default values where relevant.

tasks.title: Documentation command usage inventory add
acceptance_criteria: All major docs (README, setup docs, golden path, demo flows, developer entrypoints) are scanned for command snippets.
Each snippet is classified as user-path, dev-fallback, historical or invalid.
Invalid snippets are linked to exact files/lines and mapped to fix tasks.

tasks.title: Doc-vs-code decision matrix add
acceptance_criteria: Every mismatch has one explicit decision: update_docs, update_code, update_both, or defer.
Each decision includes rationale whether implementation should follow documented intent or docs should reflect current behavior.
High-risk decisions include follow-up verification/test implications.

tasks.title: OpenAI init flag mismatch in bootstrap path fix
acceptance_criteria: Bootstrap docs and installer next-step output no longer advertise invalid `--base-url` for `ananta init` openai-compatible setup.
Examples use the parser-supported flag contract consistently (`--endpoint-url`) or an explicit alias contract from DOC-T05.
Bootstrap docs tests validate the corrected command form.

tasks.title: Backward-compatible init URL flag policy implement
acceptance_criteria: Policy for `--base-url` compatibility is explicit: either supported as alias with deprecation guidance or clearly rejected with actionable migration message.
Behavior is covered by tests.
Docs reflect the chosen compatibility policy.

tasks.title: User-facing next-step command wording align
acceptance_criteria: Default user guidance from CLI output uses `ananta ...` command forms.
Developer fallback docs remain explicit in `docs/cli/developer_entrypoints.md` and are not removed.
Tests/snapshots are updated to match intended user-path output.

tasks.title: CLI-first vs Docker-first onboarding split clarify
acceptance_criteria: README and setup docs explicitly separate CLI local-dev path (no Docker requirement) from full UI/Compose path.
No section claims contradictory defaults for the same audience/context.
Cross-links route users to the correct path by intent (CLI usage vs full stack).

tasks.title: Python minimum version contract align
acceptance_criteria: Single minimum Python version is agreed and consistently represented across packaging, doctor output and docs.
If different minimums are intentional by runtime mode, docs explain the split explicitly.

tasks.title: Hub fallback provenance vocabulary reconcile
acceptance_criteria: Canonical docs and runtime metadata use the same `execution_mode` and `fallback_reason` semantics.
If legacy values remain for compatibility, aliases are explicitly documented and tested.
Blocked fallback state semantics are documented consistently with runtime behavior.

tasks.title: Architecture source-of-truth mapping add
acceptance_criteria: Core architecture claims are mapped to concrete code modules and tests.
Mapping covers queue ownership, delegation boundaries, fallback behavior and verification/artifact flow.
Unmapped claims are marked as aspirational, not current implementation.

tasks.title: Historical vs active architecture labeling normalize
acceptance_criteria: Each architecture doc has explicit lifecycle label (canonical, active-supporting, historical snapshot).
Historical docs point to canonical replacements.
No silent removals; migration notes preserve traceability.

tasks.title: Code-vs-description architecture decision review
acceptance_criteria: Report explicitly states where code should change to match architecture description versus where docs should be corrected.
Each item references impacted modules and risk if left unchanged.
Follow-up tasks are added for all P0/P1 mismatches.

tasks.title: Documentation command contract tests add
acceptance_criteria: Tests fail when docs advertise unknown CLI commands or unsupported flags.
Tests explicitly cover high-risk snippets from bootstrap and quickstart flows.
Developer fallback docs are validated against compatibility entrypoints separately from user-path docs.

tasks.title: Docs reference and track inventory validation add
acceptance_criteria: Tests catch stale todo file references and broken canonical doc pointers.
Track inventory reflects actual active files and track IDs.
Known consolidated docs remain valid pointers.

tasks.title: CLI help dependency coupling assessment and fix
acceptance_criteria: Decision is explicit whether `ananta --help` should run without full backend dependency install.
If yes, imports are refactored to lazy-load command modules and tests cover minimal-help startup.
If no, docs/install flow states dependency precondition unambiguously.

tasks.title: Demo docs default path normalize
acceptance_criteria: Demo flows use official user-path command examples by default.
Developer/internal fallback examples are clearly separated and labeled.
Command examples remain reproducible and aligned with guided modes.

tasks.title: Documentation track inventory integration add
acceptance_criteria: Documentation reconciliation track is listed in active track inventory with accurate scope.
Inventory no longer claims incorrect `todo.json` track identity.

tasks.title: Release gate documentation drift hook add
acceptance_criteria: Release gate can run documentation drift checks in report mode first, strict mode optional.
Failure output identifies concrete file and contract mismatch.
Gate does not block unrelated builds when docs drift check is disabled by profile.

execution_order_summary: Build executable command and docs usage inventories from code/test reality first.
Create an explicit decision matrix before changing docs or code for each mismatch.
Fix bootstrap/CLI contract drift and onboarding contradictions next.
Reconcile fallback/architecture semantics between docs and runtime metadata.
Add automated tests and release-gate hooks so drift does not reappear.
summary_notes: This todo intentionally prioritizes evidence-based reconciliation from code and tests over broad doc rewrites.
Deletion is not the default action for stale docs; lifecycle labeling plus canonical pointers comes first.
Where architecture text and code differ, this track requires an explicit choose-and-justify decision instead of silently editing whichever is easier.
